title: Koyamacalia levingei
taxonomy: Koyamacalia levingei is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the Himalayan region. This plant is classified as a member of the subfamily Cichorioideae, within the tribe Cremanthodaeae. Further classification includes it as part of the genus Koyamacalia. Koyamacalia levingei is closely related to other species within the same genus, particularly Koyamacalia formosana.
uses: Koyamacalia levingei has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic activities, which may contribute to its potential medicinal applications. In some Asian cultures, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential benefits. Further studies are required to confirm the efficacy and safety of Koyamacalia levingei for medicinal use.

intro: Koyamacalia levingei, also known as Koyamacalia or K. levingei, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of K. levingei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, K. levingei is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various folk remedies and supplements.
